document: Guidelines on Environmental Risk Management Insurers Dec 2020-2 (Singapore - Row 14)

sections.heading: INTRODUCTION
sections.title: Introduction to Environmental Risk Management Guidelines for Insurers
sections.summary: These Guidelines aim to enhance insurance sector resilience to environmental risks through sound risk management practices. They apply to all insurers operating in Singapore under the Insurance Act, including those via foreign insurer schemes. The Guidelines cover underwriting and investment activities exposed to material environmental risks. Insurers must implement these practices proportionally to their business size and risk profile, with MAS allowing group-level application for locally-incorporated insurers.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes the scope and applicability of environmental risk management for insurers, emphasizing the need for tailored approaches based on business size and risk profile. It clarifies that the Guidelines apply to underwriting and investment activities with material environmental exposure.

requirements.text: Guidelines apply to all insurers, including those under foreign insurer schemes in Singapore
Guidelines apply to underwriting and investment activities with material environmental risk exposure
Insurers must implement guidelines proportionally to business size and risk profile
Group-level application allowed for locally-incorporated insurers

sections.source_quote: These Guidelines aim to enhance the insurance sector's resilience to and management of environmental risk through setting out sound risk management practices. The Guidelines apply to all insurers, including insurers carrying on business in Singapore under a foreign insurer scheme established under Part IIA of the Insurance Act (Cap. 142).
sections.relevance_reason: No data protection or cross-border transfer relevance

sections.heading: SCOPE
sections.title: Scope of Environmental Risk for Insurers
sections.summary: Environmental risk encompasses climate change, biodiversity loss, pollution, and land use changes. These risks pose financial and reputational threats through physical and transition channels. Physical risks involve weather events and long-term environmental changes, while transition risks relate to policy shifts and technological changes. Insurers must assess how these risks impact their specific business models and customer profiles.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines environmental risks relevant to insurers, emphasizing physical and transition risks that can affect financial stability. It highlights the need for insurers to evaluate their specific exposure to climate-related and biodiversity risks.

requirements.text: Environmental risk includes climate change, biodiversity loss, pollution, and land use changes
Physical risk arises from weather events and long-term environmental changes
Transition risk arises from adjustments to environmentally sustainable economies
Environmental risks impact insurers through financial and reputational channels

sections.source_quote: Environmental risk arises from the potential adverse impact of changes in the environment on economic activities and human well-being.
sections.relevance_reason: No data protection or cross-border transfer relevance

sections.heading: GOVERNANCE AND STRATEGY
sections.title: Governance and Strategic Oversight for Environmental Risk
sections.summary: The Board and senior management must integrate environmental risk into business strategies and risk appetite frameworks. They are responsible for approving environmental risk management frameworks, ensuring material risks are addressed in risk appetite, and establishing clear roles for oversight. Insurers must maintain effective oversight of environmental risk disclosures and ensure directors understand environmental risks.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates strong governance for environmental risk, requiring Board-level oversight and integration into risk appetite frameworks. It emphasizes the need for insurers to have clear accountability structures for managing environmental risks.

requirements.text: Board and senior management must incorporate environmental considerations into risk appetite and business plans
Board must approve environmental risk management framework and policies
Board must ensure environmental risk is addressed in risk appetite framework
Board must set clear roles and responsibilities for environmental risk oversight
Senior management must develop and implement environmental risk management framework
Senior management must review effectiveness of environmental risk framework regularly

sections.heading: Integrating Environmental Issues
sections.title: Integrating environmental risk into underwriting processes
sections.summary: Underwriters must use external and proprietary sources to evaluate environmental impacts of transactions and collaborate with experts to improve data quality. Insurers must develop internal guidance for environmental risk detection and escalation, embedding these processes within their underwriting policies. Environmental risk appetite and procedures may require customization to align with existing risk management frameworks.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires insurers to assess environmental risks during underwriting, including customer's risk management capacity and climate transition impacts, which affects cross-border data transfers if environmental risk assessments involve international data flows.

requirements.text: Underwriters should check potential environmental impact of transactions using publicly available and proprietary sources
Insurers should develop internal guidance for environmental risk detection and escalation
Environmental risk appetite and processes may require greater customisation to fit existing risk management processes

sections.source_quote: Underwriters should be provided with the means to check the potential impact of the proposed transaction on the environment, from both publicly available and proprietary sources, and work with external experts to enhance the quality of data collected to better understand a customer's environmental risk profile.
sections.relevance_reason: Tangential governance for environmental risk in underwriting

sections.heading: Ongoing Monitoring
sections.title: Ongoing environmental risk monitoring for investments
sections.summary: Insurers must continuously monitor environmental risks across investments and portfolios, using both macro and granular approaches. They should track environmental impacts, develop metrics for risk assessment, and reassess investments following significant environmental events or regulatory changes. Insurers must document and escalate material environmental risks through internal processes.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandates continuous environmental risk monitoring for investment portfolios, which impacts data transfers if environmental risk assessments require cross-border data flows or international climate data.

requirements.text: Insurers should monitor potential and actual environmental impact on investments and portfolios on an ongoing basis
Insurers should consider environmental impacts from both macro and granular asset selection perspectives
Insurers should develop metrics to indicate environmental risk in investment portfolios
Insurers should monitor evolution of environmental risk metrics to assess effectiveness of actions
Insurers may use proprietary information, research, models, and ratings to inform portfolio analysis
Insurers should reassess risk and return profiles following significant environmental events or regulatory changes
Insurers must escalate material environmental risk exposures through internal processes

sections.source_quote: Insurers should put in place appropriate processes and systems to monitor, assess and manage the potential and actual impact of environmental risk on individual investments and portfolios on an ongoing basis, where material.
sections.relevance_reason: Tangential: environmental risk monitoring not directly data compliance

sections.heading: DISCLOSURE
sections.title: Environmental risk disclosure requirements
sections.summary: Insurers must disclose their environmental risk management approach annually to stakeholders, including quantitative metrics on sectoral exposures. Disclosures should align with international frameworks like TCFD, covering governance, strategy, risk management, and metrics. Insurers must review disclosures regularly to ensure comprehensiveness and relevance.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires insurers to disclose environmental risk management practices, which may involve cross-border data transfers if disclosures include international climate data or financial metrics.

requirements.text: Insurers should disclose environmental risk management approach annually to stakeholders
Disclosures should include potential impact of material environmental risk on the insurer
Disclosures should align with TCFD recommendations on governance, strategy, risk management, and metrics
Insurers should review disclosures regularly to improve comprehensiveness and relevance

sections.source_quote: The insurer should, at least on an annual basis, disclose its approach to managing environmental risk in a manner that is clear and meaningful to its stakeholders.
sections.relevance_reason: No data protection or cross-border transfer relevance
